pattern: With so few people, every possible split of the group into good and bad can be tried directly, keeping only the splits where every good person's claims hold up.
hints: A person is either always honest or possibly lying, so any candidate answer is really just a yes/no label for each person -- and with at most 15 people there are only a few thousand such labelings to check.

Represent a candidate labeling as a bitmask over the people; for a labeling to be valid, every person marked 'good' in that mask must have every one of their statements agree with the mask itself (a bad-marked person's statements are never checked).
Enumerate every bitmask from all-bad to all-good, discard the ones that fail the agreement check for any good person's statements, and take the one with the most set bits among the survivors.

edge_inputs.description: smallest size, two people, mutual bad accusations
no one says anything about anyone
everyone accuses everyone else of being bad, all could be bad and consistent
a chain of good vouches that must all resolve together
one person calls everyone else good, contradicted by another
largest size, fifteen people, all statements neutral

stress.description: fifteen people with a dense web of random accusations and vouches, forcing the full 2^15 subset scan
stress.note: n is pinned at the real maximum of 15 so the reference exercises the full 2^15 enumeration under time pressure
